story_assessments.story_title: OpenAI’s scorecard is a sign that AI is becoming a process tool
story_assessments.target_audience: Creators, small-business operators, and team leads using AI in repeatable workflows
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want to know whether AI actually improves a process instead of just producing more drafts and cleanup
story_assessments.interest_signal: OpenAI is framing AI with management metrics like useful work completed and cost per successful task
story_assessments.stop_reason: It turns AI hype into a practical test that anyone with a workflow can understand quickly
story_assessments.promised_payoff: A simple way to tell whether AI belongs in a repeatable task before you scale it
story_assessments.proof_point: OpenAI said companies should measure useful work completed, cost per successful task, dependability, and whether each AI dollar buys more work at scale
search_phrases: how to test AI in a workflow
story_assessments.score_reason: Broadly useful to creators and small teams, easy to explain, and strongly tied to a measurable workflow decision.

story_assessments.story_title: GPT-Red points to a future where safety checks are built into the pipeline
story_assessments.target_audience: Product teams, ops leads, security-conscious AI users, and developers connecting AI to tools
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want to reduce the risk that an AI system takes the wrong action when it can access files, browsers, or business tools
story_assessments.interest_signal: OpenAI said it trained GPT-Red and used it to adversarially train GPT-5.6 against prompt injection
story_assessments.stop_reason: The phrase prompt injection plus the idea of a model hardening itself creates an immediate risk/control hook
story_assessments.promised_payoff: A clear reminder to add a human approval step before irreversible AI actions
story_assessments.proof_point: OpenAI said it trained GPT-Red, an automated internal red-teaming model, and used it to adversarially train GPT-5.6 to improve resistance to prompt injection
story_assessments.score_reason: Strong operational relevance and a simple safeguard make it easy to understand even for non-specialists.

story_assessments.story_title: Google’s ad labels show that disclosure is becoming part of the production process
story_assessments.target_audience: Marketers, creators, small brands, and ad operations teams
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They need a repeatable way to label AI-made ads and keep their asset records organized
story_assessments.interest_signal: Google is moving AI disclosure into Search, YouTube, and Discover with a built-in panel and mandatory labels
story_assessments.stop_reason: It affects a familiar creator and small-business workflow that many people already have to manage
story_assessments.promised_payoff: A practical system for keeping prompts, edits, and disclosure together so assets can be reused cleanly
story_assessments.proof_point: Google said it is adding a 'How this ad was made' panel in My Ad Center on Search, YouTube and Discover, and that advertisers must label ads created or edited with generative AI
story_assessments.score_reason: Very strong for marketers and creators because the policy change maps directly to a routine asset-management problem.

story_assessments.story_title: ASML’s higher outlook suggests the AI budget problem is still infrastructure-led
story_assessments.target_audience: Founders, operators, finance leads, and infrastructure watchers
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want to plan AI spending without treating it like a one-time experiment
story_assessments.interest_signal: Reuters reported that ASML raised its 2026 sales forecast and plans to expand capacity by 30% in each of the next two years
story_assessments.stop_reason: It connects AI demand to budgets, chips, and capacity planning rather than abstract model chatter
story_assessments.promised_payoff: A reason to budget AI as recurring operating spend and tie it to completed work
story_assessments.proof_point: Reuters reported that ASML raised its 2026 sales forecast and said it would expand capacity by 30% in each of the next two years because of strong demand linked to AI chips and data-center buildout
story_assessments.score_reason: Useful for planning and budgeting, but narrower and less immediately actionable than the workflow and disclosure stories.

story_assessments.story_title: OpenAI’s teen-safety changes hint that consumer AI will need household controls
story_assessments.target_audience: Parents, educators, and consumer AI product teams
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want AI tools that feel safer and easier to manage for teens and families
story_assessments.interest_signal: OpenAI said it strengthened teen protections, rolled out age prediction, and expanded parental controls
story_assessments.stop_reason: It speaks directly to household safety, a clear and recognizable concern
story_assessments.promised_payoff: A signal that consumer AI is moving toward age-based defaults and visible governance tools
story_assessments.proof_point: OpenAI said it had strengthened default teen protections, rolled out age prediction, expanded parental controls, and will keep adding age-appropriate safeguards in the coming months
story_assessments.score_reason: Clear audience relevance and a concrete governance angle make it easy to discuss without needing technical background.

tiktok.story_title: OpenAI’s scorecard is a sign that AI is becoming a process tool
tiktok.target_audience: Creators, small-business operators, and team leads using AI in repeatable workflows
tiktok.opening: Is your AI saving time or just making drafts?
tiktok.payoff: OpenAI said companies should measure useful work completed, cost per successful task, dependability, and how much cleanup the human still does. For one repeatable job, track time in, time out, error rate, and revision time. If the cleanup wipes out the savings, the workflow is not helping.
tiktok.selection_reason: This is the cleanest quick-hit story for a broad audience because it turns AI hype into a simple before-and-after test.

youtube.story_title: GPT-Red points to a future where safety checks are built into the pipeline
youtube.target_audience: Product teams, ops leads, security-conscious AI users, and developers connecting AI to tools
youtube.opening: Could one prompt trigger the wrong action?
youtube.payoff: OpenAI said it trained GPT-Red and used it to harden GPT-5.6 against prompt injection. The practical takeaway is to add human approval before AI sends money, publishes publicly, or exposes private data.
youtube.selection_reason: The risk-and-control angle is concrete, searchable, and easy to explain in a short format.

facebook.story_title: Google’s ad labels show that disclosure is becoming part of the production process
facebook.target_audience: Marketers, creators, small brands, and ad operations teams
facebook.opening: Do you keep disclosure with every AI-made ad?
facebook.payoff: Google said it will add a 'How this ad was made' panel in My Ad Center on Search, YouTube and Discover, and advertisers must label ads created or edited with generative AI. For small teams, the prompt, edit history, and disclosure line should live with the asset so it can be reused cleanly later.
facebook.selection_reason: This fits a familiar creator workflow and invites a practical yes/no or process-based reply from people who manage ads and visuals.

pinterest.story_title: OpenAI’s scorecard is a sign that AI is becoming a process tool
pinterest.target_audience: Creators, small-business operators, and team leads using AI in repeatable workflows
pinterest.opening: How to Build an AI Workflow Scorecard for Small Teams
pinterest.payoff: Measure one repeatable task end to end so you can see time saved, errors introduced, and cleanup needed before you scale AI. Save this checklist for your next workflow test, and comment with the task you want to measure first.
pinterest.selection_reason: Pinterest rewards searchable how-to framing, and this turns a broad AI concept into a practical checklist people can save and reuse.

linkedin.story_title: ASML’s higher outlook suggests the AI budget problem is still infrastructure-led
linkedin.target_audience: Founders, operators, finance leads, and infrastructure watchers
linkedin.opening: Are you budgeting AI as a tool or an operating expense?
linkedin.payoff: Reuters reported ASML raised its 2026 sales forecast and said it will expand capacity by 30% in each of the next two years because demand tied to AI chips and data-center buildout remains strong. That suggests AI should be planned like recurring spend, not a one-off experiment.
linkedin.selection_reason: It frames AI as a budgeting and planning issue, which matches LinkedIn’s operator and leadership audience.

overall_reasoning: Each story was assessed independently for audience fit, practical consequence, searchability, usefulness, novelty, visual potential, discussion potential, and short-form clarity. The strongest platform matches were chosen separately rather than forcing one story everywhere: the workflow scorecard for TikTok and Pinterest, the safety pipeline story for YouTube Shorts, ad disclosure for Facebook, and infrastructure budgeting for LinkedIn. All social assets stay inside the verified research pack and keep the tone practical, calm, and human-led.
